summary: Correctness hardening + LMP/futility pruning. A/B-validated; 1 proposal rejected with data.

kept.name: TT XOR-key + zero-key sentinel
kept.impact: SMP race-safety: rejects torn writes from concurrent threads. Empty slots no longer collide with hash=0.

kept.name: TT aging (generation counter)
kept.impact: Stale entries from earlier moves of the same game are freely replaceable; deep results from move N-30 stop crowding out fresh ones from move N.

kept.name: Forced-move shortcut
kept.impact: If only one legal move exists at root, return immediately. Saves the entire clock budget for the next decision.
kept.ab_result: n/a (no-op on bench positions, real win in forced recaptures)

kept.name: Late Move Pruning
kept.impact: At depth<=4, skip late quiet moves entirely (well-ordered list rarely hides the best move late). Threshold: searched >= 3 + depth*depth.
kept.ab_result: fixed-depth bench: -44.3% wall time, -62% nodes. Timed bench: +4 plies depth (+3.1%) at 2s budget. Best-move check: 5/8 same, 1 strictly better (queen vs rook promo), 2 reasonable alternates.

kept.name: Frontier futility pruning
kept.impact: At depth==1, skip quiet moves when static_eval + 200cp <= alpha (can't plausibly raise alpha). First move always searched.
kept.ab_result: -2.4% wall time, -4.5% nodes vs LMP-only. 8/8 same best move.

kept.name: Hash UCI option max raised to 4096 MB
kept.impact: Was 512; prod uses 384 so was already near cap. Lets us scale up if a future Pi has more RAM.

rejected.name: Qsearch TT probe + store
rejected.reason: Depth-fixed bench was favorable (-9% time), but timed bench at 500ms showed -13% depth reached. Root cause: Pi 4's slow RAM makes the extra probe-per-qnode (60-80% of all nodes) expensive, and the strict 'depth>=' replacement rule prevents qsearch's depth=0 entries from actually storing in most slots. Net negative on this hardware.
